summary: Iter15 polish + extraction tested end-to-end. Backend: 8/8 pytest cases pass (cancel endpoint, idempotency, audit row, dismissed-plan filter, active-missions regression). Frontend: all 4 spec items verified — chat thread is 864px tall (>=720), 5 'AUTONOMY L#' chips render on rail tiles, cortex-mission-cancel button shows on running missions and is hidden on cancelled, 5 mission-cancel-{id} mini-buttons render on /dashboard/missions, and CommandCenter subcomponent extraction (ChatMessage/Composer/PhaseIndicator/MemorySearch) regressed nothing — composer renders, memory-search modal opens on Ctrl+K, active mission rail still populates.

ui_bugs.issue: Pressing Ctrl+K on /dashboard appears to open BOTH the memory-search modal AND the global Quick-find command palette at the same time (screenshot shows the 'Search past conversations…' input mounted at the top while the centered 'Type a command or search…' palette is also open). The two share the same keybinding. Memory-search worked correctly in isolation; this is a keybinding collision regression to investigate.
Console warning: 'DialogContent requires a DialogTitle for the component to be accessible for screen reader users.' Wrap title with VisuallyHidden or add a DialogTitle.

action_items: (Optional) Resolve Ctrl+K keybind collision so only the memory-search modal opens from CommandCenter (or only one of the two modals at a time).
(Optional, a11y) Add DialogTitle (or VisuallyHidden wrapper) to MemorySearch DialogContent.
critical_code_review_comments: missions.py /cancel endpoint correctly inserts mission_events audit row (label='cancelled' surfaces in /api/cortex/missions/{id}/events). Idempotency guard returns 400 with clear message; 404 path works.

cortex_active_missions.py exposes mission_events.event under the 'label' field (with _→space). Any downstream consumer expecting raw 'event' key needs to use 'label' instead — already correct in current code, just a callout.

cortex_recommendations.py timedelta filter respects 7d window; matches both opportunity.type AND opportunity.intent against rec_type as designed. Confirmed end-to-end (post-dismiss: target type removed from /console/opportunities).
test_credentials: session_token cookie + Authorization: Bearer test_session_1779636592168 (user_test1779636592168). Found at /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Created (and cleaned up) several TEST_iter15 missions for cancel-flow verification. Also cleared the user's cortex_dismissed_plans collection at start of dismiss-filter test (left empty for next run; engine will repopulate naturally).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter15 backend file = /app/backend/tests/test_cortex_iter15.py (pytest, 8 tests). The cancel endpoint is idempotent — re-running tests is safe. NOTE: TestDismissedPlanFilter writes one row to cortex_dismissed_plans; if you re-run repeatedly without clearing, the user may end up with all opportunity types dismissed and the test will skip (no opps left to dismiss). To reset: db.cortex_dismissed_plans.delete_many({'user_id': 'user_test1779636592168'}). Active-mission events endpoint returns event under 'label' key (mission_events.event with _→space).
rca of the issue: N/A — all spec items pass. Two minor UX observations noted above for main agent to consider (Ctrl+K collision, missing DialogTitle).
